name: Mojon Blanco - circular from Mancha Real
name_original: Mojón Blanco - circular desde Mancha Real
region_intro: Hiking around Mancha Real offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. The region is characterized by dramatic limestone mountains, deep gorges, and picturesque valleys. Surrounding the town, extensive olive groves provide a contrasting scenery for walks. This varied topography supports a rich biodiversity, making it an engaging environment for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Mancha Real The most popular hiking route is Cerro Almadén Loop Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and views typical of the Sierra Mágina foothills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mojon Blanco - circular from Mancha Real, a difficult 10.1 miles (16.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through rugged mountain landscapes. Local hikers also love the Geodetic Vertex – Non-Cyclable Trail Section loop from Pegalajar, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Mancha Real is defined by limestone mountains, deep gorges, and extensive olive groves. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents. The routes in Mancha Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Mancha Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Mancha Real?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes available around Mancha Real, offering a variety of experiences from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park and its surroundings.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on hikes near Mancha Real?
A: Hikes around Mancha Real feature diverse landscapes, including dramatic &lt;strong&gt;limestone mountains&lt;/strong&gt;, deep gorges, and picturesque valleys within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. Closer to the town, you'll also find extensive &lt;strong&gt;olive groves&lt;/strong&gt;, characteristic of the Jaén province.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking trails in Mancha Real?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy hiking trail available around Mancha Real. These trails are generally well-suited for those looking for a less strenuous outdoor experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Mancha Real?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987865932/cerro-almaden-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Almadén Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987855306/mojon-blanco-circular-from-mancha-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojon Blanco - circular from Mancha Real&lt;/a&gt; offers a more difficult challenge through rugged mountain landscapes.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking near Mancha Real?
A: The region offers several notable natural attractions. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4754151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;the Entrance to the Sierra Mágina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, the environmental interest zone of Peña del Águila, and the intriguing Cueva de los Murciélagos (Cave of the Bats). The highest point in Jaén province, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt;, is also a prominent feature.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Mancha Real suitable for families?
A: Many of the moderate trails, especially those with varied terrain and scenic views, can be suitable for families with some hiking experience. It's always recommended to check the specific route's distance and elevation gain to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Mancha Real?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on many trails within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, but it's advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. Always check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mancha Real?
A: The best time for hiking in Mancha Real is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, especially in the lower olive groves, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, particularly at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any trails with significant viewpoints or panoramic vistas?
A: Yes, many trails in the Sierra Mágina Natural Park offer stunning viewpoints. Routes that ascend to higher elevations, such as those near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5314248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojón Blanco&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5314271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Almadén&lt;/a&gt;, provide expansive panoramic views of the dramatic limestone mountains and surrounding landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Mancha Real?
A: The hiking experiences in Mancha Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the dramatic mountain scenery, and the opportunity to explore the rich biodiversity of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987855306/mojon-blanco-circular-from-mancha-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojon Blanco - circular from Mancha Real&lt;/a&gt; are classified as difficult. These trails feature significant elevation gain and rugged mountain landscapes, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads from Mancha Real?
A: While Mancha Real is a gateway to the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, public transport options directly to specific trailheads can be limited. It's often recommended to use a personal vehicle or local taxi services to access more remote starting points for hikes.

Q: Where can I park my car when going for a hike in Mancha Real?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting directly from Mancha Real, you can often find street parking. For trails deeper within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, designated parking areas may be available near popular access points, though these can be limited.
tips: Peak where the municipalities of Pegalajar, Mancha Real and Torres meet, and from which there are spectacular views of the Bercho shade, one of the steepest and wildest areas in the municipality of Pegalajar. We can also enjoy incredible views of the Sierra de Jaén.
